title: Red Sox’ Trevor Story undergoes sports hernia surgery
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Trevor Story underwent successful sports hernia surgery, missing an estimated 6-10 weeks, impacting Boston Red Sox's roster.
Story's injuries since joining Red Sox include 2024 shoulder surgery and 2023 elbow surgery, affecting his performance.
With Story out, Boston utilizes Isaiah Kiner-Falefa, Andruw Monasterio, and potentially Marcelo Mayer at shortstop.

title: Peter Wolf left one Boston art school to form the J. Geils Band. Six decades later, he earned an honorary doctorate from another.
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Peter Wolf receives an honorary doctorate from Massachusetts College of Art and Design, decades after leaving art school for music.
Wolf's 2025 memoir, 'Waiting on the Moon,' highlights his colorful past, including encounters with art and music icons.
Wolf plans to release a new album and potentially write more books, expanding on his artistic pursuits.

title: Boston 25 hires Lauren Walsh for newly-created role
author: Chad Finn
bullets: Lauren Walsh has been hired as a sports media journalist for Boston 25 in a newly-created role.
Walsh, a Medford native, previously worked for WSMV in Nashville and WXII in Winston-Salem.
Walsh is a Syracuse graduate and former NESN intern, marking a return to Boston for the journalist.

title: Somerville restaurant owner says viral ‘pool water’ reply to customer review was unauthorized
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Olivia's Kitchen owner, Simona Sbano, disavows viral response to a negative review, stating it was unauthorized and the employee was disciplined.
The restaurant had faced criticism for comments about Somerville tap water and its liquor license status on the Reddit platform.
Olivia's Kitchen will now serve tap water upon request and asserts it meets safety and health standards, addressing customer concerns.

title: With split vote, City Council effort to reject Wu’s budget fails
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Boston City Council's motion to reject Mayor Michelle Wu's budget proposal failed with a 6-6 vote.
Councilor Brian Worrell led the effort to reject the budget, viewing it as a leverage tool for amendments.
Wu's budget includes a $47 million city deficit due to heavy snowfall and a $22.8 million BPS deficit.

title: Kevin McHale questions Celtics’ 3-point heavy approach: ‘The ball has got to touch the paint’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Kevin McHale critiques Celtics' reliance on 3-pointers, advocating for more inside play and ball movement.
Boston Celtics led the league in 3-point attempts but struggled in points in the paint and free-throws.
McHale admires Tatum and Brown, hoping they can secure another title together with improved strategies.

title: Mass. pols issue report on possible effects of Haiti TPS termination on local health care
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Massachusetts politicians report that ending Haiti TPS could worsen healthcare workforce shortages and compromise patient care quality.
The proposed termination of TPS would affect approximately 45,000 Haitians in Massachusetts, many of whom work in healthcare.
Healthcare providers report increased staff shortages and service disruptions, fearing the loss of experienced Haitian workers.

title: This New England town hosts one of America’s best 4th of July celebrations, according to USA Today readers
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Bristol, R.I., hosts the 5th best 4th of July celebration in the U.S., according to USA Today's 2026 readers' poll.
Bristol's celebration, ongoing since 1785, includes concerts, parades, and draws over 200,000 attendees annually.
USA Today ranked City of Pittsburgh Independence Day Celebration as the top 4th of July event in America.

title: England use a Beatles soundtrack to reveal controversial World Cup squad picks
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: England's World Cup squad was revealed via a video featuring the Beatles' song 'Come Together.'
High-profile players like Trent Alexander-Arnold and Phil Foden were left out of the squad by manager Thomas Tuchel.
Surprises in the squad include Ivan Toney, despite limited playtime under Tuchel, and injured Tottenham full-back Djed Spence.

title: Stephen Colbert bids farewell to ‘Late Show’ with star-studded finale
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Stephen Colbert ended 'The Late Show' after 11 years, with Paul McCartney as his final guest.
The finale featured several celebrity appearances, including Bryan Cranston, Paul Rudd, and Neil deGrasse Tyson.
The show ended with a musical performance and a nod to 'St. Elsewhere' with the Ed Sullivan Theater shrinking into a snow globe.
